title: Front Desk Agent Daily Procedures
purpose: Establish standardized opening, mid-day, and closing procedures for the front desk agent role at the Wastewater Operations department to ensure consistent service, accurate record-keeping, and proper handoff between shifts.
scope: Applies to all personnel assigned to front desk coverage at the Wastewater Operations administrative office during business hours (Monday through Friday, 7:00 AM to 4:00 PM).

definitions.definition: A documented request for field service issued through the county work order management system.
The physical or digital register where all non-employee visitors are recorded upon entry.
Any incoming call received outside business hours and routed to the on-call duty officer.

responsibilities.owns: All procedures defined in this SOP, including opening, visitor management, call handling, and closing.
Reviewing the daily visitor log and call log at end of day, signing off on completion.
Handling after-hours escalations forwarded by the front desk.

steps: Unlock the front desk area and turn on workstation and printer.
Log into the county network using assigned credentials.
Open the work order management system, visitor log, and email client.
Review any overnight emails and flag urgent items for the supervisor.
Check the answering service log for messages received after hours.
Greet every visitor within 15 seconds of arrival.
Verify the visitor's name, affiliation, and purpose of visit.
Record entry in the visitor log: name, time in, organization, contact being visited.
Issue a visitor badge and direct the visitor to the appropriate waiting area.
Notify the staff member the visitor is here to see.
Record time out and collect the visitor badge upon departure.
Answer all calls within three rings using the standard greeting: 'Wastewater Operations, [your name] speaking, how may I help you?'
Determine whether the call is informational, a service request, or an emergency.
For service requests, capture caller name, address, contact number, and issue description.
Create a work order in the county system for any field service request.
For emergencies (sewer overflow, hazardous condition), follow the emergency escalation procedure in WW-SOP-003.
Log every call in the daily call log with time, caller, and disposition.

procedure.phase: End of Day Closing (3:30 PM – 4:00 PM)
steps: Reconcile the visitor log: ensure all visitors have a recorded time out.
Complete the daily call log summary.
Forward the office phone line to the after-hours answering service.
Submit the daily log to the shift supervisor for signoff.
Lock the front desk area and secure all materials.

exceptions: If the work order management system is unavailable, record service requests on the paper backup form and enter them as soon as the system is restored.
If a visitor refuses to sign the visitor log, politely deny entry and notify the shift supervisor.
If an after-hours emergency call comes in before the line is forwarded, follow WW-SOP-003 and document the incident.
